fields.summary: p99 latency spike on pay-svc payment authorization
fields.description: Automated alert: p99 on POST /v1/payments/authorize in pay-svc jumped from ~310ms to 4.2s starting 14:33Z. Began roughly 1 min after the 14:32Z deploy to ecom-payments. Connection acquisition from the userdata pool appears to be the bottleneck.

comments.author: spatel
comments.body: pay-svc is holding way more DB connections than its allocation. Looks deploy-related.

fields.summary: user_store primary connection pool exhausted (max_connections hit)
fields.description: userdata-primary active_connections = 100/100, connection_refused_rate > 0. One client is consuming the headroom. Refused connections are now hitting other clients including authn.

comments.author: jordan.lee
comments.body: Grouping connections by app: payment_service_v2 is holding 61 connections (allocation is 20). authn is starved and getting connection-refused.

fields.summary: authn token validation timing out — platform-wide login failures
fields.description: auth_service token validation failing closed because session lookups against userdata exceed the 2s query_timeout. Logins and refreshes erroring across the board. authn itself is healthy — the dependency (the user data store) is refusing connections.

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: This is not an AuthService bug. authn cannot get connections to the shared DB. Symptom, not cause.

fields.summary: api-gw 503/401 error storm on authenticated routes
fields.description: edge-gw returning 503 on routes that require auth, plus 401s where token validation fails open-loop. Error rate at 78%. Root cause is downstream (authn cannot validate), but the gateway is where customers see it.

comments.author: sam.patel
comments.body: gateway is just propagating downstream failures. Enabling load-shed to protect what's left.

fields.summary: Checkout completely failing — orders + cart-svc cannot complete purchase
fields.description: Customers cannot complete checkout. orders fails at the payment step because payments errors out, and cart-svc sessions are dropping. Estimated revenue impact accruing. This is the customer-facing face of the incident.

comments.author: m.rodriguez
comments.body: Checkout success rate is 6%. Escalating to SEV1.

fields.summary: SEV1: Storefront checkout down — cascading failure from payments to auth
fields.description: Declaring SEV1. Incident Commander: mrodriguez. Cascade: a payments deploy leaked DB connections, exhausted the shared user data store, starved authn, and took down checkout via api-gw. Child tickets: INC-4471, INC-4472, INC-4473, INC-4474, INC-4475.

comments.author: mrodriguez
comments.body: All hands. Need the payments tech lead to confirm the suspect deploy ASAP.

fields.summary: Investigation: payment_service_v2 not releasing connections under error
fields.description: Heap + pool dump shows payment_service_v2 acquires a connection during authorize, makes a new synchronous read against the user data store, and on PSP timeout the connection is never returned to the pool. Under load this leaks until the pool and then the DB headroom are consumed. The new sync read also points at userdata-primary instead of the replica.

comments.author: a.chen
comments.body: Confirmed leak path. This was introduced in a recent PR to pay-service. Pulling the diff.

fields.summary: Root cause: PR #847 in pay-service introduced pool change + leaking sync read
fields.description: PR #847 (pay-service repo), authored by a.chen and merged at 14:30Z, did three harmful things: (1) raised maximumPoolSize 20 -> 60, (2) removed connectionTimeout, (3) added a blocking userdata read on the authorize path that targets the primary, not the replica, and leaks on PSP error. Combined, this lets pay-svc consume the entire shared DB budget.

comments.author: priya.nair
comments.body: That pool change should have required UserDB owner review. It didn't get one.

comments.author: jlee
comments.body: Agreed. A pool/config change to a shared DB client needs sign-off from the user-db owner.

fields.summary: Roll back PR #847 and redeploy previous payments build
fields.description: Reverting PR #847 in payments-svc and redeploying the last known-good build of payments. Do NOT raise max_connections on the primary as a workaround per the runbook — that masks the leak.

comments.author: achen
comments.body: Rollback live. pay-svc connection count dropping toward its 20 allocation.

fields.summary: Retry storm (thundering herd) amplifying load through api-gw
fields.description: Even after the pay-svc rollback started, gateway request volume is 5x normal because failed clients are aggressively retrying. This keeps userdata under pressure and delays recovery of authn. Keeping load-shed on at api-gw until pools normalize.

comments.author: sam.patel
comments.body: Back-pressure holding. Recovery will be gradual, not instant.

fields.summary: shopping_cart sessions dropped during the outage window
fields.description: Because token validation via authn was failing, shopping_cart could not associate sessions with accounts and dropped in-progress carts. Need a recovery comms note for affected customers.

fields.summary: notif-svc backlog — order confirmation emails delayed
fields.description: The few orders that did complete queued confirmation emails in notifications, but the backlog built up while upstream was failing. Queue will drain on its own once orders recovers; no action beyond monitoring.

fields.summary: Partial recovery — auth-layer stabilizing as DB pool drains
fields.description: Post-rollback, userdata connections falling below 85. authsvc token validation success rate climbing back toward normal. Login errors dropping. Not fully clear yet due to residual retry load.

comments.author: pnair
comments.body: auth_service p99 validation back under 60ms. Almost there.

fields.summary: Full recovery confirmed across pay-svc, authn, and user-db
fields.description: All green. pay-svc holding 18/20 connections (normal). user-db active_connections back to ~40/100. authn validation nominal. Checkout success rate restored to 99%+. Closing the SEV1.

fields.summary: Post-incident review: shared user-database exhaustion from payment_service_v2 PR #847
fields.description: Summary: PR #847 (authored by Alex C.) merged to the ecom-payments repo raised the pool size, removed the connection timeout, and added a leaking synchronous read against the user-database primary on the payments authorize path. payment_service_v2 (a.k.a. pay-svc) consumed the entire shared DB budget, starving auth-layer and cascading to a full checkout outage. Total customer impact: ~76 minutes. Action items below.
customfield_action_items: Enforce a hard per-client connection cap at the user-database side, not just in app config.
Require user-db owner (Jordan Lee) review on any PR touching shared-DB pool config.
Add a CI check that fails PRs raising maximumPoolSize or removing connectionTimeout in payment-service.
Route payments reads to the replica; block primary reads on the authorize path.
Add exponential backoff + jitter at api-gw to prevent retry storms.

comments.author: Alex C.
comments.body: Owning this. The pool change in pay-svc should never have shipped without UserDB owner review. Action items drafted.

comments.author: jordan.lee
comments.body: +1. The shared user-database is the real constraint here; app-side caps alone are not enough.
